content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Government Code Section 84214                Date : March 28, 1979

From : Barbara Campbell
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

Subject: Conversation with Ken Ito, Los Angeles City Clerk's Office

Ken Ito of the Los Angeles City Clerk's Office
called today to ask if it was acceptable to send mail-
grams as notifications for late contributions. I said
the staff had been advising mailgrams were allowed and
that advice had been endorsed by the Commission.

He said that they have received a number of mail-
grams more than 48 hours after they were sent. I said
that the mailgrams were acceptable and that he should
use the date indicated on the mailgram as the date of
receipt in the City Clerk's Office.

sections.question_synthetic: Are mailgrams acceptable as notifications for late contributions, and how should the date of receipt be determined when mailgrams arrive more than 48 hours after they were sent?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Mailgrams are acceptable for late contribution notifications, and the date indicated on the mailgram itself should be used as the date of receipt in the City Clerk's Office, regardless of when the mailgram physically arrives.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter documenting a phone conversation between FPPC staff (Barbara Campbell) and Ken Ito of LA City Clerk's Office. No formal question/conclusion sections present; information extracted from conversational narrative. Commission had previously endorsed this mailgram advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are mailgrams acceptable as notifications for late contributions, and how should the date of receipt be determined when mailgrams arrive more than 48 hours after they were sent?

CONCLUSION: Mailgrams are acceptable for late contribution notifications, and the date indicated on the mailgram itself should be used as the date of receipt in the City Clerk's Office, regardless of when the mailgram physically arrives.

I said the staff had been advising mailgrams were allowed and that advice had been endorsed by the Commission. He said that they have received a number of mail- grams more than 48 hours after they were sent. I said that the mailgrams were acceptable and that he should use the date indicated on the mailgram as the date of receipt in the City Clerk's Office.
embedding.summary: FPPC staff advises that mailgrams are acceptable for notifying late contributions, and that the date on the mailgram should be used as the receipt date, even if physical delivery occurs more than 48 hours after sending.
